surface_verification.justification: Adds a diff-coverage job to two adopt CI templates (shared/templates/github-actions/) + a convention-test update. No target-app runtime surface. The templates are validated by shared/tests/test_ci_workflow_convention.py + the adopt scaffolder test; the runtime pattern itself was proven end-to-end on WebUI PR #205 (identical client/server vitest+diff-cover, merged green in real CI).

behaviors.behavior: the nextjs (flat) template ships a warn-only diff-coverage job running pinned diff-cover@10.3.0 --fail-under=80
the vite (client/server) template ships the same warn-only diff-coverage job feeding both packages' cobertura reports
behaviors.evidence: same test (iterates all vitest templates); both templates parse + carry the job

behaviors.behavior: the diff-coverage job is Linux-only (ubuntu-latest, excluded from the cross-platform OS-matrix convention)
behaviors.evidence: TestCITemplatesDiffCoverageGate asserts runs-on==ubuntu-latest + PLATFORM_AGNOSTIC_JOB_PATTERNS includes diff-coverage so TestCITemplatesCrossPlatformMatrix skips it; both PASSED

behaviors.behavior: both templates remain valid YAML, keep their matrix/dormant/permissions invariants, and the adopt scaffolder still copies them
the scaffolded pattern actually reports real diff-coverage end-to-end on a real client/server repo
behaviors.evidence: WebUI PR #205 (b449fbe) — the identical client/server vitest+diff-cover pattern merged green in WebUI's real CI (Diff coverage job SUCCESS; locally 100% over a real 6-commit range, both packages resolved)
